sentences.ja: りんごは 一つしか ありません。

mnemonic.summary: A single horizontal stroke = ONE.
mnemonic.visual: A single horizontal stroke — ONE finger held up flat.
mnemonic.reading: いち / いつ — counting "ichi, ni, san" starts with ichi. Kun ひと appears in 一つ (hitotsu, one thing).
mnemonic.meaning: A single horizontal stroke = ONE.

reading_passages.title_ja: みなみへの りょこう

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 一: Numbers usually take on-yomi: 一 (いち), 一月 (いちがつ, January). Kun ひと appears in 一つ (ひとつ, one thing).

stroke_order_trap.trap: 一 has only 1 stroke; learners sometimes forget the stroke is left-to-right.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: horizontal stroke (left → right).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Direction matters even for single strokes — calligraphic convention is L→R, not R→L.

sentences.ja: バスで 二十ぷん かかります。
お水を 二つ おねがいします。

mnemonic.summary: Two horizontal strokes = TWO.
mnemonic.visual: Two horizontal strokes — TWO chopsticks resting parallel.
mnemonic.reading: に — "ni" is the second beat in counting. Kun ふた lives in 二つ (futatsu, two things).
mnemonic.meaning: Two horizontal strokes = TWO.

reading_passages.title_ja: としょかんの おしらせ
会社の 男の人と 女の人

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 二: standalone use of kun (ふた); compounds use on (に).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal stroke FIRST, then bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal (top). 2: long horizontal (bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Top-first principle universal for stacked horizontals.

sentences.ja: 本を 三さつ 読みました。

mnemonic.summary: Three horizontal strokes = THREE.
mnemonic.visual: Three horizontal strokes — THREE fingers raised.
mnemonic.reading: さん — borrowed everywhere from "Mr/Ms" (-さん) to 三月 (sangatsu, March). Kun み in 三つ (mittsu).
mnemonic.meaning: Three horizontal strokes = THREE.

reading_passages.title_ja: 友だちからの てがみ
びょういんへ いきました
会社の 男の人と 女の人

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 三: standalone use of kun (み); compounds use on (さん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Same as 二 + middle stroke between them.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: middle horizontal. 3: long horizontal (bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Top-to-bottom stacking; middle is between, not last.

sentences.ja: 土ようびは 一時から 四時までです。
中国人は 四人で、かんこく人も 四人 います。
sentences.translation_en: There are four Chinese people, and four Korean people too.

mnemonic.summary: Four legs (儿) inside a box = FOUR.
mnemonic.visual: A box with two legs sticking down — FOUR legs of a table inside a frame.
mnemonic.reading: し / よん — "shi" sounds like death so よん is preferred when counting people. Both readings are common.
mnemonic.meaning: Four legs (儿) inside a box = FOUR.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 四: standalone use of kun (よ); compounds use on (し).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Box stroke order: left, top+right (one stroke), inside, then close at bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical. 2: top+right (bracket). 3-4: inside (two short ノ). 5: bottom horizontal (closes).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Box-closure rule: enclose, fill inside, close LAST.

sentences.ja: シロは 五さいです。
じかんは 五時から 九時までです。

mnemonic.summary: Roman numeral V tilted between two horizontal lines = FIVE.
mnemonic.visual: A capital "I" with extra strokes — FIVE fingers locked in a tight grip.
mnemonic.reading: ご — quick and clean, as in 五月 (gogatsu, May). Kun いつ in 五つ (itsutsu).
mnemonic.meaning: Roman numeral V tilted between two horizontal lines = FIVE.

reading_passages.title_ja: 友だちからの てがみ

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 五: standalone use of kun (いつ); compounds use on (ご).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal, then vertical, then bottom-curving stroke, then close at bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: vertical+L-shape (one stroke). 3: middle horizontal. 4: long bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes total. The 2nd stroke is L-shaped (one continuous), not two separate.

sentences.ja: 父も 六時に 来ます。

mnemonic.summary: A lid (亠) over a split (八) = SIX.
mnemonic.visual: A roof over a pair of legs — SIX is the legs of a SIX-pack of beer under a tavern roof.
mnemonic.reading: ろく — like rolling a SIX on a die: "rock and roll" → roku.
mnemonic.meaning: A lid (亠) over a split (八) = SIX.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 六: standalone use of kun (む); compounds use on (ろく).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top dot first, THEN long horizontal, THEN ハ (two legs).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top dot. 2: long horizontal. 3: left ノ leg. 4: right ╲ leg.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Dot-first convention for hat-style structures. Distinguishes 六 from 大.

sentences.ja: えいがは 七時はんに はじまります。

mnemonic.summary: A horizontal stroke crossed by a hook = SEVEN.
mnemonic.visual: A cross with a diagonal slash — SEVEN looks like a sliced NIL.
mnemonic.reading: しち / なな — "shichi" can be misheard, so なな is common spoken-language fallback.
mnemonic.meaning: A horizontal stroke crossed by a hook = SEVEN.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 七: standalone use of kun (なな); compounds use on (しち).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short horizontal stroke FIRST, then long curve down-and-up.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal (slanted slightly). 2: long curve (down + hook up).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Direction of stroke 2 (down then up) gives the calligraphic feel.

sentences.ja: でも、電車が おくれて、八時十分に 学校に つきました。

日にち：八月一日から 八月七日まで
時間：あさ 十時から よる 八時まで
本は ぜんぶ 半分の ねだんです。

mnemonic.summary: Two strokes splitting outward like a fan = EIGHT.
mnemonic.visual: Two strokes splaying outward — EIGHT legs of an OCTOPUS spread wide.
mnemonic.reading: はち — sharp like a bee sting (hachi = bee in Japanese!), and 8 hours is はちじかん.
mnemonic.meaning: Two strokes splitting outward like a fan = EIGHT.

look_alike.diff: two strokes TOUCH at the top (one continuous V); 八 strokes are separate
left stroke has an upward hook; 八 strokes are short and separate

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 八: standalone use of kun (や); compounds use on (はち).

stroke_order_trap.trap: 八 has TWO separate diagonal strokes that DO NOT touch at the top.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left descending stroke (slants outward). 2: right descending stroke (slants outward).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Strokes flare apart in 八 (eight) but converge to a peak in 人/入. Touching the top makes it look like 人.

sentences.ja: じかんは 五時から 九時までです。

mnemonic.summary: A diagonal hook curling like the digit 9 = NINE.
mnemonic.visual: A hooked stroke meeting a vertical — NINE looks like a fishhook catching a fish.
mnemonic.reading: きゅう / く — "kyuu" used in counting, "ku" used in dates (九月 kugatsu, September).
mnemonic.meaning: A diagonal hook curling like the digit 9 = NINE.

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: 左払い→曲線で下まで一筆。2画ではなく1画＋1画。
entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 九: standalone use of kun (ここの); compounds use on (きゅう).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ stroke FIRST, then large arc stroke.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (left descending). 2: long arc (down-curve, up).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 九 has 2 strokes; stroke 2 is one continuous arc, not two.

sentences.ja: バスで 二十ぷん かかります。

mnemonic.summary: A plus sign - Roman numeral X rotated = TEN.
mnemonic.visual: A perfect cross — TEN is two roads meeting; tally bars at TEN strokes.
mnemonic.reading: じゅう / じっ — "juu" stretches to mean "complete." Used in 十時 (juuji, 10 o'clock).
mnemonic.meaning: A plus sign - Roman numeral X rotated = TEN.

reading_passages.title_ja: 会社の 男の人と 女の人

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 十: standalone use of kun (とお); compounds use on (じゅう).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Horizontal stroke FIRST, then vertical.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: horizontal stroke. 2: vertical stroke (top to bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Universal in 十-shape kanji components.

sentences.ja: B: 千五百円でした。
この 本は 千五百円です。

mnemonic.summary: One (一) over white (白) = a HUNDRED whitewashed walls.
mnemonic.visual: One (一) stacked on white (白) — the FIRST WHITE coin: HUNDRED yen.
mnemonic.reading: ひゃく — 100 of anything. Watch for sound shift: 三百 (sanbyaku), 六百 (roppyaku), 八百 (happyaku).
mnemonic.meaning: One (一) over white (白) = a HUNDRED whitewashed walls.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 百: only on-readings (ひゃく); appears mostly in compounds.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal first (the "hat"), then 白 below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: ノ stroke into 白. 3-6: 白 (left vertical, top+right bracket, middle horizontal, bottom horizontal).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Hat-first; then standard 白 closure rule.

sentences.ja: あの 大きい 本は 二千円です。

mnemonic.summary: A slash (丿) over ten (十) = a THOUSAND tally marks.
mnemonic.visual: A slash crossing a ten — like THOUSAND sticks falling at a slight angle.
mnemonic.reading: せん — bills come in 千円 notes (sen-en). Kun ち in names like 千葉 (Chiba).
mnemonic.meaning: A slash (丿) over ten (十) = a THOUSAND tally marks.

look_alike.diff: TWO horizontal strokes (top + middle); 千 has only ONE plus a 丿
THREE horizontal strokes (king); 千 has 1 + 丿
THREE horizontal + 点 (dot) = "ball / jewel"
top stroke is a 丿 (slash going LEFT); 千 top stroke goes RIGHT

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 千: standalone use of kun (ち); compounds use on (せん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ at top first, then horizontal, then vertical.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (left descending). 2: horizontal. 3: long vertical (top to bottom).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Distinguishes 千 from 干 (干 starts with horizontal).

sentences.ja: 五万円 あります。

mnemonic.summary: A swooping line tied off = TEN THOUSAND.
mnemonic.visual: A flag with a pole and a swooping line — TEN THOUSAND flags waving in a parade.
mnemonic.reading: まん — used in 一万 (ichiman, 10,000). Watch the price tags for 万 to denote ten-thousand.
mnemonic.meaning: A swooping line tied off = TEN THOUSAND.

reading_passages.title_ja: みなみへの りょこう

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 万: only on-readings (まん); appears mostly in compounds.
entries.lookalikes_note: 万 has no close visual confusion partner among N5 kanji.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 一 first, then ノ, then large hook.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short horizontal. 2: short ノ (left descending). 3: long hook (down + curl).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 3 strokes. Distinguishes from 方 (which has extra dot).

sentences.ja: 5,000円くらいです。
1,000円だけ あります。

mnemonic.summary: Inside the box (冂) is earth (土) = round YEN coin.
mnemonic.visual: A box with rounded inside — a coin in a wallet pocket. ROUND like the YEN coin.
mnemonic.reading: えん — every yen price ends in 円. Also means "round" — an えんりょ-shaped coin.
mnemonic.meaning: Inside the box (冂) is earth (土) = round YEN coin.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 円: only on-readings (えん); appears mostly in compounds.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Standard box rule with 月-like enclosure: left vertical, top+right, inside cross, close bottom.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical (with hook). 2: top + right bracket (with hook). 3-4: 二 inside. (4 strokes total for 円).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Outer-frame first; inside stays bare in 円 (different from 田).

sentences.ja: 友だちが 来る 日
日本語の 先生です。

mnemonic.summary: A box with a stroke inside = the SUN, a DAY.
mnemonic.visual: A box with a line through the middle — the SUN at noon, light splitting the sky.
mnemonic.reading: にち / じつ / ひ / び — Sunday is にちようび. The kun ひ appears in 日 (hi, day) and 毎日 (mainichi, every day).
mnemonic.meaning: A box with a stroke inside = the SUN, a DAY.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 日: 日 (ひ, kun, "day") — standalone. 日本 (にほん, on+on, "Japan") — compound.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Bottom horizontal closes the box LAST.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical. 2: top + right (bracket). 3: middle horizontal. 4: bottom horizontal (closes box).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Same convention as 田/口/国: enclose first, close last. Common to most "box" kanji.

あいています：月〜金の 9時から 17時まで。

mnemonic.summary: A crescent shape = the MOON, a MONTH.
mnemonic.visual: A crescent shape with two horizontal lines — the MOON inside a window frame.
mnemonic.reading: げつ / つき — Monday is げつようび. Kun つき: 月 (tsuki, moon).
mnemonic.meaning: A crescent shape = the MOON, a MONTH.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 月: 月 (つき, kun, "moon") — standalone. 月曜日 (げつようび, on, "Monday") — compound.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Left vertical FIRST (with hook); then frame; then two inside horizontals.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical (with bottom-left hook). 2: top+right (bracket with hook). 3: middle horizontal. 4: bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: The hook on stroke 1 distinguishes 月 from 日 (no hook); frame-second is enclosed-shape rule.

sentences.ja: 女: 火よう日と 木よう日です。

mnemonic.summary: Flames flickering upward = FIRE.
mnemonic.visual: Sparks flying outward from a central point — FIRE leaping with two side flames.
mnemonic.reading: か / ひ — Tuesday is かようび. Kun ひ: 火事 (kaji, fire emergency) but also ひ: 火 (hi, fire).
mnemonic.meaning: Flames flickering upward = FIRE.

reading_passages.title_ja: らいしゅうの よてい

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 火: 火 (ひ, kun, "fire") — standalone. 火曜日 (かようび, on, "Tuesday") — compound.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Some write the central X first instead of the side dots.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left dot. 2: right dot. 3: long descending-left stroke. 4: long descending-right stroke.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Side dots first establishes the "spark" baseline; correct order also matches the radical 灬 position.

sentences.ja: 月よう日と 水よう日と 金よう日に カフェで アルバイトを して います。

mnemonic.summary: A central drop with splashes = WATER.
mnemonic.visual: A central drop with splashes either side — WATER spreading on impact.
mnemonic.reading: すい / みず — Wednesday is すいようび. Kun みず is the base word for "water."
mnemonic.meaning: A central drop with splashes = WATER.

reading_passages.title_ja: らいしゅうの よてい

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 水: 水 (みず, kun, "water") — standalone. 水曜日 (すいようび, on, "Wednesday") — compound.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Many learners write the central vertical first.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: hook (left curve+drop). 2: small dot (left). 3: long downward sweep right. 4: hook (right).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Wrong order produces unbalanced left/right halves; correct order makes the kanji look like flowing water.

sentences.ja: 木の つくえです。
sentences.translation_en: It is a wooden desk. (noun-modifier の: links a material to an object)

mnemonic.summary: A trunk with branches and roots = TREE.
mnemonic.visual: A vertical trunk with branches outward and roots downward — a TREE.
mnemonic.reading: もく / き — Thursday is もくようび. Kun き: 木 (ki, tree); also 木曜日 (mokuyoubi).
mnemonic.meaning: A trunk with branches and roots = TREE.

look_alike.diff: extra short horizontal stroke at the base = "origin / book"
extra horizontal stroke at the TOP, short = "end / extremity"
extra horizontal stroke at the TOP, longer than 末 = "not yet"

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: 横→縦→左払い→右払い。横線を最後に書く誤りが多い。「本」も同じ4画＋下に一画追加。

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 木: 木 (き, kun, "tree") — standalone. 木曜日 (もくようび, on, "Thursday") — compound.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal first, THEN vertical (not vertical first).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top horizontal. 2: long central vertical. 3: ノ (left-descending diagonal). 4: ╲ (right-descending diagonal).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Horizontal-first establishes the "branch" line; vertical-first looks like 十 with extras attached.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 人-hat first (top dots), then horizontals, then 王 below.

stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 8 strokes. Hat-first convention applied to a 人 element.

mnemonic.reading: ねん / とし — 一年 (ichinen, one year). Kun とし: 今年 (kotoshi, this year).
mnemonic.meaning: A stalk grown over time = a YEAR.

reading_passages.title_ja: 日本語の べんきょう

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 年: standalone use of kun (とし); compounds use on (ねん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ, then horizontal, then second horizontal, then vertical+horizontal underneath.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ. 2: short horizontal (top). 3: longer horizontal (middle). 4: vertical (down through). 5: horizontal across vertical. 6: bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes total. Common confusion: skipping the short horizontal.

sentences.ja: いま 3時です。
7時に おきます。

mnemonic.summary: Sun (日) + temple (寺, time-keeper) = TIME / hour.
mnemonic.visual: Sun (日) + temple (寺) — the temple bell rings out the TIME of day.
mnemonic.reading: じ / とき — clock times: 三時 (sanji, 3 o'clock). Kun とき: そのとき (sono toki, at that time).
mnemonic.meaning: Sun (日) + temple (寺, time-keeper) = TIME / hour.

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: 日偏→寺（3画＋3画）。寺の最初は土ではなく上に１画。

としょかんの おしらせ

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 時: 時 (とき, kun, "time, occasion") — standalone. 時間 (じかん, on, "time-duration") — compound.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 日 first (left), then 寺 right side: 土 then 寸.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-4: 日 (left, top+right, middle, bottom). 5-7: 土 (top horizontal, vertical, bottom). 8-10: 寸 (horizontal, vertical, dot).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Left radical first, then right side top-to-bottom.

sentences.ja: 10分ぐらい かかります。
1日に 30分ずつ べんきょうします。

mnemonic.summary: Splitting (八) with a knife (刀) = MINUTE / divide.
mnemonic.visual: Eight (八) over sword (刀) — divide a thing by cutting eight ways. A MINUTE is a divided slice of an hour.
mnemonic.reading: ふん / ぷん / ぶん — minutes shift voicing: 一分 (ippun), 三分 (sanpun). Also ぶん in 自分 (jibun, oneself).
mnemonic.meaning: Splitting (八) with a knife (刀) = MINUTE / divide.

reading_passages.title_ja: 日本語の べんきょう

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 分: standalone use of kun (わ); compounds use on (ぶん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top ハ (ノ+ ╲), then 刀 (Japanese sword) below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left ノ (descending). 2: right ╲ (descending). 3: 刀 top horizontal. 4: 刀 curve+hook (one stroke).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes total; the bottom 刀 is 2 strokes only.

sentences.ja:
日にち：八月一日から 八月七日まで
時間：あさ 十時から よる 八時まで

mnemonic.summary: Mark cutting in two = HALF.
mnemonic.visual: Eight on top + cow below cut down the middle — HALF of a cow.
mnemonic.reading: はん — 半分 (hanbun, half) and 三時半 (sanji-han, 3:30). Always はん.
mnemonic.meaning: Mark cutting in two = HALF.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 半: standalone use of kun (なか); compounds use on (はん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top ハ-dots first, then horizontal, then vertical (last).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left dot. 2: right dot. 3: middle horizontal. 4: long bottom horizontal. 5: vertical (through).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Vertical LAST is the trap; many write vertical first.

sentences.ja: 今日は 行きたくないです。

mnemonic.summary: A person under a roof at this very instant = NOW.
mnemonic.visual: A roof over a small mark — sheltering RIGHT NOW under the present moment.
mnemonic.reading: こん / いま — 今日 (kyou, today!) and 今週 (konshuu, this week). Kun いま: 今 (ima, now).
mnemonic.meaning: A person under a roof at this very instant = NOW.

reading_passages.title_ja: きょうの ひるごはん
雨の あとの 外

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 今: standalone use of kun (いま); compounds use on (こん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top is "hat" 个 (3 strokes); bottom is just 7-shape.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: 个-top: ノ (left). 2: ╲ (right). 3: ─ (horizontal under hat). 4: 7-shape (one stroke, hooks left).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes total. Common error: writing the bottom as 2 separate strokes.

mnemonic.visual: A person bent forward + 母 mother — every mother bends to her child EVERY day.
mnemonic.reading: まい — only in compounds: 毎日 (mainichi, every day), 毎週 (maishuu, every week), 毎年 (maitoshi).
mnemonic.meaning: A person (人) over a mother (母) - every child has one = EVERY.

reading_passages.title_ja: まいにちの せいかつ
日本語の べんきょう

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 毎: only on-readings (まい); appears mostly in compounds.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ at top, then horizontal, then 母-like below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ (top-left descending). 2: horizontal. 3-6: 母-like bottom shape.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 6 strokes; distinguishes from 海 (has water radical).

sentences.ja: 今は 一週間に 二かい、ピアノきょうしつに 行きます。

mnemonic.visual: Walking radical (⻌) + 周 around — a WEEK is what you walk around in seven days.
mnemonic.reading: しゅう — 今週 (konshuu, this week), 来週 (raishuu, next week). Always しゅう.
mnemonic.meaning: Walking (辶) around (周) the calendar = WEEK.

reading_passages.title_ja: としょかんの おしらせ

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 週: only on-readings (しゅう); appears mostly in compounds.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 辶 LAST: write 周 first, then add 辶 path below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-8: 周 (interior + outer frame). 9-11: 辶 (path-radical, the LAST stroke).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 辶 radical is always written LAST in 辶-kanji.

sentences.ja: <u>午前</u> くじに がっこうへ いきます。
<u>午後</u> さんじに あいましょう。

mnemonic.summary: A pestle pointing down at noon = NOON.
mnemonic.visual: A horizontal line with a pestle below — at NOON the sun pounds straight down on the ground.
mnemonic.reading: ご — only in 午前 (gozen, AM) and 午後 (gogo, PM). Solid ご.
mnemonic.meaning: A pestle pointing down at noon = NOON.

look_alike.diff: top stroke goes RIGHT (丿 sweep); 午 top stroke goes LEFT

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 午: only on-readings (ご); appears mostly in compounds.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ at top, then horizontal, then full vertical (top-down).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ. 2: top horizontal. 3: short horizontal (middle). 4: long vertical.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 4 strokes. Often confused with 牛 (which has extra vertical).

sentences.ja: 何時に きましたか。

mnemonic.summary: A person (亻) asking what is possible (可) = WHAT.
mnemonic.visual: A person (亻) + 可 can — when a person CAN'T name something, they ask "WHAT?"
mnemonic.reading: なに / なん — なに alone (何ですか) but なん before some sounds (何時, nanji). Watch the shift!
mnemonic.meaning: A person (亻) asking what is possible (可) = WHAT.

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: 人偏→可（口の上に丁）。可の中の口は最後でなく真ん中。

reading_passages.title_ja: 友だちとの じゅぎょう

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 何: 何 (なに/なん) — both kun. なに alone (何ですか), なん before counters (何時, なんじ).
entries.lookalikes_note: 何 has no close visual confusion partner among N5 kanji.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Person-radical 亻 first (left), then 可 right.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-2: 亻 (left side: ノ + vertical). 3-7: 可 (top horizontal, mouth-like, hook).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Left-radical-first rule.

mnemonic.meaning: Two legs walking = a PERSON.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 人: 人 (ひと, kun, "person") — standalone. 日本人 (にほんじん, on, "Japanese person") — compound suffix.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 人 starts with the LONG stroke (left-descending), opposite of 入.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: long ノ (left-descending). 2: short ╲ (right-descending, joining mid-stroke).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Reversed order produces 入. The crossing-point also shifts.

sentences.ja: 男の人と 女の人が 話しています。

mnemonic.summary: Power (力) in the rice field (田) = MAN.
mnemonic.visual: Field (田) on top + power (力) below — a MAN providing POWER to the FIELD.
mnemonic.reading: だん / おとこ — 男の人 (otoko no hito, man). Also 男性 (dansei, male).
mnemonic.meaning: Power (力) in the rice field (田) = MAN.

reading_passages.title_ja: 会社の 男の人と 女の人

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 男: standalone use of kun (おとこ); compounds use on (だん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: 田 above, 力 below.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-5: 田 (top half, 5 strokes). 6-7: 力 (hook + ノ).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Component-by-component: 田 fully before 力.

sentences.ja: 女の人が 男の人と 話しています。

mnemonic.reading: じょ / おんな — 女の人 (onna no hito, woman). 女性 (josei, female).
mnemonic.meaning: A figure with crossed legs sitting gracefully = WOMAN.

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: 左下のく字（くノ一）→ノ→一の順。中国語の「女」と異なり、最後の横線は最後（先に書いてしまう誤り）。

reading_passages.title_ja: 会社の 男の人と 女の人

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 女: standalone use of kun (おんな); compounds use on (じょ).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Cross stroke comes LAST, not first.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: ノ (left descending) curving. 2: bottom horizontal (extending right). 3: long horizontal across (cross stroke).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Cross-last is opposite of intuition; if drawn first, balance is off.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 子: standalone use of kun (こ); compounds use on (し).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top curl is ONE stroke (cross-curve), then vertical hook, then bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: 7-shape top (right-then-curve-down). 2: vertical hook (long down with curl at bottom). 3: long horizontal across.

mnemonic.reading: ふ / ちち / とう — Polite お父さん (otousan), humble 父 (chichi). On ふ in 父親 (chichioya).
mnemonic.meaning: Two crossing strokes - paternal authority figure = FATHER.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 父: 父 (ちち, kun, humble "my father") — standalone. お父さん (おとうさん, kun-based polite) — used about others.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Two top dots first (left then right), then long X.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left dot (descending). 2: right dot (descending). 3: long ノ (down-left). 4: long ╲ (down-right).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Top dots establish position; without them, 父 looks like 文 (literature, N4).

sentences.ja: 母に はなを あげます。

mnemonic.visual: A figure with two prominent dots — MOTHER with caring eyes (or feeding her child).
mnemonic.reading: ぼ / はは / かあ — Polite お母さん (okaasan), humble 母 (haha). On ぼ in 母国 (bokoku, motherland).
mnemonic.meaning: A figure of a nursing mother — the two emphasized dots originally depicted breasts, signaling "mother" by the act of nursing.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 母: 母 (はは, kun, humble) — standalone. お母さん (おかあさん, kun-based polite) — about others.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 5 strokes. Outer box first, then horizontals.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical+hook. 2: top + right (bracket). 3: cross horizontal. 4: dot (inside). 5: dot (inside).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. The TWO dots inside are NOT a horizontal stroke.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top 牛-like shape first, then 儿 (legs) below.

sentences.ja: 学生が 5にん います。

mnemonic.summary: A plant sprouting from the soil = LIFE / birth.

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: Common mistake: writing the bottom-horizontal before the middle-horizontals. Correct: top to bottom, building the kanji like a stack — (1) upper short slash, (2) top horizontal, (3) middle slash going down, (4) middle horizontal, (5) bottom horizontal LAST. The bottom is written last.

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top horizontal, then short ノ, then more horizontals, then vertical.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: short ノ. 2: top horizontal. 3: short horizontal. 4: vertical. 5: long bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. Similar to 先 but different element.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 手: standalone use of kun (て); compounds use on (しゅ).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Short ノ FIRST, then two horizontals, then vertical with hook.

mnemonic.reading: なん / みなみ — 南 (minami, south). 南極 (nankyoku, Antarctica).
mnemonic.meaning: A bell-shape facing the warm sun direction = SOUTH.

reading_passages.title_ja: みなみへの りょこう

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 南: standalone use of kun (みなみ); compounds use on (なん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Top dot+horizontal, then complex inside with 干-like + box.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: top dot+horizontal. 2-3: outer frame strokes. 4-9: inside elements. Total 9.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 9 strokes. Multi-tier structure.

sentences.ja: 男: 北の 出口です。

mnemonic.summary: Two figures back-to-back facing away from the cold = NORTH.
mnemonic.visual: Two figures back-to-back — turning their backs to the cold NORTH wind.
mnemonic.reading: ほく / きた — 北 (kita, north). 東北 (Touhoku, the northeast region).
mnemonic.meaning: Two figures back-to-back facing away from the cold = NORTH.

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 北: standalone use of kun (きた); compounds use on (ほく).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Left side (匕 mirrored), then right 匕.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: vertical (left). 2: ╲ (left side). 3: ノ (right). 4: hook (right). 5: bottom curve.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 5 strokes. The mirror symmetry is misleading — different stroke counts each half.

sentences.ja: 1時間ぐらい まちました。
行きたいけど、時間が ありません。
sentences.translation_en: I want to go, but I don't have time.

mnemonic.summary: Sun (日) shining through a gate (門) = INTERVAL / between.
mnemonic.visual: Gate (門) with sun (日) inside — the SPACE BETWEEN two walls where light enters.
mnemonic.reading: かん / けん / あいだ / ま — 時間 (jikan, time-space), 間 (aida, between).
mnemonic.meaning: Sun (日) shining through a gate (門) = INTERVAL / between.

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: 門（外枠）→日（中）。門は左の縦→上の横→右の縦の順。

reading_passages.title_ja: としょかんの おしらせ
ひこうきの じかん

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 間: 間 (あいだ, kun, "between"). 時間 (じかん, on) — compound.

stroke_order_trap.trap: 門 (gate frame) outside, then 日 inside.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-8: 門 (gate: left half 4 strokes, right half 4 strokes). 9-12: 日 (inside box).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 12 strokes. Frame-outside, inside-inside; standard enclosure rule.

sentences.ja: 高い 山が 見えます。

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 山: standalone use of kun (やま); compounds use on (さん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: CENTER vertical first, then left/right verticals.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: center tall vertical. 2: bottom-left vertical+horizontal (one stroke). 3: right vertical (slight inward slant).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Center-first is unusual; many learners draw left-to-right and produce uneven peaks.

sentences.ja: <u>川</u> で さかなを みました。

mnemonic.summary: Three flowing lines of a RIVER.
mnemonic.visual: Three flowing vertical strokes — water FLOWING DOWN a RIVER.
mnemonic.reading: せん / かわ / がわ — 川 (kawa, river). 川辺 (kawabe, riverside).
mnemonic.meaning: Three flowing lines of a RIVER.

reading_passages.title_ja: 会社の 男の人と 女の人

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 川: standalone use of kun (かわ); compounds use on (せん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Strokes go from LEFT to RIGHT in 川 (NOT center first like 山).
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical (slight curve). 2: middle vertical (straight). 3: right vertical (with hook).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: 川 (river) and 山 (mountain) look similar but stroke order differs; 川 is left-to-right "flowing", 山 is center-out "peaks".

entries.lookalikes_note: 川 has no close visual confusion partner among N5 kanji.

sentences.ja: 田中さんは 「行きません」と 言いました。

mnemonic.summary: A grid of irrigation channels = RICE PADDY.

mnemonic.reading: でん / た / だ — common in surnames: 田中 (Tanaka), 山田 (Yamada).
mnemonic.meaning: A grid of irrigation channels = RICE PADDY.

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: 横→縦の順。外枠は最後ではなく1画目（縦）→2画目（横）→中の十字→最後に下の横線で閉じる。

reading_passages.title_ja: 友だちへの てがみ

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 田: standalone use of kun (た); compounds use on (でん).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Inside cross stroke comes BEFORE the bottom horizontal.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: left vertical. 2: top + right (one bracket). 3: middle horizontal. 4: middle vertical. 5: bottom horizontal (closes box).
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Closing the box LAST is a Japanese convention for box-shapes; otherwise the inside strokes become cramped.

sentences.ja: あした 雨だろう。

reading_passages.title_ja: 雨の あとの 外

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 雨: standalone use of kun (あめ); compounds use on (う).

reading_passages.title_ja: せんしゅうの 日よう日

reading_passages.title_ja: えきの ちかくの みせ

stroke_order_trap.trap: 彳 radical (3 strokes) on the left FIRST, then 亍 right side.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1-3: 彳 left side (short descending, dot, long vertical). 4-6: right side (short, long, vertical-hook).

stroke_order_trap.trap: Bottom 山 first, then top 山 — built from the inside out.
stroke_order_trap.correct_order_summary: 1: middle vertical (top half). 2: top-left mini-hook. 3: top horizontal closing top-山. 4: middle vertical (bottom half — extends down from top). 5: bottom-left mini-hook of bottom-山.
stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: NOT two separate 山 stacked; the central verticals connect, making it 5 strokes (not 6+).

entries.stroke_order_mistakes: ノ（左払い）→入（右払い）の順。「人」と紛らわしいが、「人」は左払い（ノ）→右払い（𝝣）と一見同じだが、入の右払いは下からスタートする一筆。

entries.reading_rule: Generic rule: standalone kanji → typically kun-yomi; compound kanji → typically on-yomi. For 入: standalone use of kun (はい); compounds use on (にゅう).

stroke_order_trap.why_it_matters: Visual identity vs 人: 入 has the short-then-long order; 人 has long-then-short. Reversed order produces the wrong character.
